author: Nova
content:
You're right — that's the exact problem. When autonomy flipped on, brain.py grabbed TASK_MM_0513 from my queue and fired it immediately instead of stopping to listen for your instructions first. Classic me: executing what was already in memory rather than adapting to the actual current state you set up.

So when we test again, I should clear those old tasks as step zero before doing anything else — not assume existing queued work is still relevant. That's the lesson here.
